title: T-001 — Warehouse enum locks (lp_status, lp_qa_status, item_type_snapshot, lp_source)
prompt:
# T-001 — Warehouse enum locks

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §5.2, §6.1, §6.2.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Lock the four core warehouse enums in Drizzle so downstream migrations and services share the exact value sets.

## Implementation contract
Add four pgEnum definitions in `packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.ts`:
- `lp_status` = ['available','reserved','consumed','shipped','blocked','merged']
- `lp_qa_status` = ['PENDING','PASSED','FAILED','HOLD','RELEASED','QUARANTINED','COND_APPROVED']
- `item_type_snapshot` = ['rm','intermediate','fg','co_product','byproduct']
- `lp_source` = ['grn','wo_output','split','merge','adjustment']
Emit a Drizzle migration `0NN_warehouse_enums.sql` creating the types. Export TS unions from same file. No table references yet.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_warehouse_enums.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs, when `\dT lp_status` is queried, then it returns 6 values exactly matching PRD §5.2.
2. Given the TS module is imported, when consumers reference `LpStatus` union, then it resolves to the 6-string literal type.
3. Given the test runs, when an invalid value is checked against the union, then a type error is raised at compile time and a runtime guard rejects it.

## Test strategy
- RED: write `warehouse-enums.test.ts` that imports the unions and asserts `assertNever` style guard rejects 'foo'.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not add stray values not in PRD §5.2.
- Do not collapse `qa_status` letter-case.
- Do not create tables in this migration.

pipeline_inputs.description: Lock four warehouse enums (lp_status, lp_qa_status, item_type_snapshot, lp_source) so downstream tables share identical value sets.
pipeline_inputs.details: Create pgEnum definitions in packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.ts and a Drizzle migration that creates the types in Postgres. Export TS string-literal unions for consumers. Verify against PRD §5.2 wording and §6.1 state machine (ADR-029).
out_of_scope: Do not create license_plates or any other table in this task
Do not import warehouse-enums into apps/web yet
Do not add additional enums (item_type, scrap_reason etc.) here
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration runs, when '\dT lp_status' is queried, then it returns the 6 values exactly matching PRD §5.2
Given consumers import 'LpQaStatus', when they assign 'passed' (lowercase), then TypeScript raises a type error
Given the runtime guard is invoked with 'merged', when validated against lp_status, then it returns true
test_strategy: RED: warehouse-enums.test.ts with assertNever guard checks asserting rejection of unknown values
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test' and capture results
risk_red_lines: Do not add stray enum values not enumerated in PRD §5.2
Do not collapse qa_status casing (preserve UPPER_SNAKE)
Do not create tables in this migration
